Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Mixed-Precision Quantization
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that incorporating curvature-aware precision routing into mixed-precision quantization can significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of deep learning models, particularly in resource-constrained environments. By leveraging geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies, we can develop a framework that adaptively allocates precision levels to different model components based on their curvature and sensitivity.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT have explored various aspects of mixed-precision quantization, including training-free proxies, adaptive precision allocation, and post-training quantization. However, these approaches often rely on heuristics or manual configurations, which can lead to suboptimal performance. Our proposed curvature-aware precision routing framework distinguishes itself by integrating training-free representation geometry, linear algebra, and spectral quantization theory to provide a more principled and efficient approach to mixed-precision quantization.
Abstract: This paper proposes a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework for efficient mixed-precision quantization in deep learning. By leveraging geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies, our framework adaptively allocates precision levels to different model components based on their curvature and sensitivity. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach through extensive experiments on various benchmark models and datasets, showing significant improvements in accuracy and efficiency compared to state-of-the-art mixed-precision quantization methods.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Evaluate the performance of our curvature-aware precision routing framework on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet datasets using ResNet and MobileNet models.
Experiment 2: Compare the efficiency and accuracy of our framework with existing mixed-precision quantization methods, including EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT.

Experiment 3: Investigate the impact of different curvature-aware routing strategies on the performance of our framework, including the use of different geometric subspace diagnostics and optimization proxies.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of our framework may increase the computational overhead of mixed-precision quantization.
The choice of curvature-aware routing strategy may significantly impact the performance of our framework, requiring careful tuning and evaluation.
The applicability of our framework to different model architectures and datasets may be limited, requiring further experimentation and validation.
